kpi-cards.visualPattern: Responsive grid of cards each showing a metric name label, large stat value, and inline colored change percentage on the same baseline row.
bestFor: Portfolio-level summary: NAV, daily return, and YTD return with positive/negative change coloring at a glance
Fund performance dashboard showing AUM, sharpe ratio, and max drawdown with period-over-period deltas inline
Trading desk KPI strip: P&L, open positions count, and win rate with directional change text
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when you need to show a previous-period reference value alongside the change — use kpi-card-05 or kpi-card-07 instead.

keyOptionalProps.cols: number — default: 3 — controls responsive grid columns (1, 2, 3, 4, or 5)
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — formats the stat/value before display
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of MetricItem objects. Each object uses: `name` (string, card label), `stat` or `value` (number | string, the primary big metric displayed), `change` (string, the delta text e.g. '+6.1%'), `changeType` ('positive' | 'negative' | 'neutral', drives color). The `id`, `href`, `previousStat`, `secondaryValue` fields are present in the type but not rendered by this card.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: percent: (v) => `${v}%`, currency: (v) => `$${Number(v).toLocaleString()}`, bps: (v) => `${v}bps`
kpi-cards.snippet:
<KpiCard01
  metrics={[
    { name: 'Portfolio NAV', stat: '$2.41M', change: '+1.3%', changeType: 'positive' },
    { name: 'Daily P&L', stat: '+$31,200', change: '+1.3%', changeType: 'positive' },
    { name: 'Max Drawdown', stat: '-4.2%', change: '-0.3%', changeType: 'negative' },
  ]}
  cols={3}
/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Responsive grid of cards with metric name top-left and a ring-styled colored badge (change %) top-right, large stat value below.
bestFor: Equity dashboard: ticker return badges (e.g. +12.1%) floating top-right next to metric name for instant visual scan
Risk metrics panel where a badge color (green/red/gray) immediately signals breach/no-breach status at a glance
Fund comparison table header showing relative returns as colored badges without needing a full chart
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when the change text is longer than ~8 characters — the ring badge truncates poorly; use kpi-card-01 (inline text) instead.

keyOptionalProps.cols: number — default: 3 — controls responsive grid columns (1, 2, 3, 4, or 5)
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — formats the stat/value before display
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of MetricItem objects. Each object uses: `name` (string, card label), `stat` or `value` (number | string, the primary big metric), `change` (string, shown inside badge e.g. '+12.1%'), `changeType` ('positive' | 'negative' | 'neutral', drives badge color: emerald/red/gray). The `previousStat`, `href`, `secondaryValue` fields are not rendered.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: percent: (v) => `${Number(v).toFixed(1)}%`, currency: (v) => `$${Number(v).toLocaleString()}`, bps: (v) => `${v}bps`
kpi-cards.snippet:
<KpiCard02
  metrics={[
    { name: 'AAPL Return', stat: '+18.4%', change: '+2.1%', changeType: 'positive' },
    { name: 'MSFT Return', stat: '+9.8%', change: '-0.4%', changeType: 'negative' },
    { name: 'SPY Return', stat: '+7.2%', change: '+0.6%', changeType: 'positive' },
  ]}
  cols={3}
/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Responsive grid of cards with large stat top-left, colored change text top-right, and metric name label below the stat.
bestFor: Revenue and growth summary where the headline number dominates visually and the label is de-emphasized below
Macro indicator dashboard (CPI, GDP growth, unemployment) where the current value is primary and the name is secondary
Portfolio performance overview where absolute value (e.g. $34.1K MRR) leads and the metric description follows
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when the metric name needs to be immediately recognizable before reading the value — the inverted label placement (below stat) is counter-intuitive for dense comparison panels; use kpi-card-01 instead.

keyOptionalProps.cols: number — default: 3 — controls responsive grid columns (1, 2, 3, 4, or 5)
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — formats the value/stat before display
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of MetricItem objects. Each object uses: `name` (string, label shown below stat), `value` or `stat` (number | string, the large primary metric shown top), `change` (string, colored delta shown top-right), `changeType` ('positive' | 'negative' | 'neutral'). The `previousStat`, `href` fields are not rendered.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: currency: (v) => `$${Number(v).toLocaleString()}`, percent: (v) => `${Number(v).toFixed(1)}%`
kpi-cards.snippet:
<KpiCard03
  metrics={[
    { name: 'Recurring Revenue', value: '$34.1K', change: '+6.1%', changeType: 'positive' },
    { name: 'Total AUM', value: '$500.1M', change: '+19.2%', changeType: 'positive' },
    { name: 'User Growth', value: '11.3%', change: '-1.2%', changeType: 'negative' },
  ]}
  cols={3}
/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Responsive grid of cards with name top-left, change top-right, large stat below, and an optional 'View more →' link footer separated by a border.
bestFor: Clickable MRR / ARR / user-count cards that link to detailed drill-down views within a financial dashboard
Fund-level KPI cards where each metric navigates to a fund detail page (e.g. NAV card → fund sheet)
Portfolio summary panel where each card (returns, drawdown, Sharpe) links to the corresponding analysis screen
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when none of the metrics have drill-down destinations — the empty bottom border space looks incomplete without an href.

keyOptionalProps.cols: number — default: 3 — controls responsive grid columns (1, 2, 3, 4, or 5)
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — formats the value/stat before display
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of MetricItem objects. Each object uses: `name` (string, label top-left), `value` or `stat` (number | string, large primary metric), `change` (string, colored delta top-right), `changeType` ('positive' | 'negative' | 'neutral'), `href` (string | undefined, when provided shows 'View more →' footer link). The `previousStat` field is not rendered.

    { name: 'YTD Return', value: '+14.7%', change: '-1.2%', changeType: 'negative', href: '/returns' },
  ]}

/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Responsive grid of cards with name top-left, colored change % top-right, large stat and smaller 'from {previousStat}' reference on the same row below.
bestFor: Monthly performance review: portfolio NAV vs prior month with absolute previous value shown for context
Fund AUM trend cards showing current AUM with 'from $X.XB' prior period reference inline
Trading P&L summary comparing current month vs last month with both absolute values visible
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when there is no meaningful prior-period comparison value — the 'from {prev}' label will be empty and the layout looks sparse; use kpi-card-01 instead.

keyOptionalProps.previousStat: string | number (on each MetricItem) — the prior period reference value, renders as 'from {previousStat}'

/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Responsive grid of cards with name top-left and an arrow-icon badge (▲/▼ + change %) top-right, large stat value below.
bestFor: Equity screener KPI strip where directional arrows (▲/▼) provide immediate up/down signal alongside return values
Risk dashboard showing VaR, beta, and correlation metrics with arrowhead indicators signaling direction of change
Fixed-income metrics panel (duration, yield spread, convexity) with visual up/down arrows for movement direction
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when changeType is frequently 'neutral' — the gray badge without an arrow renders as an empty square; use kpi-card-02 (text badge) instead.

The `previousStat`, `href` fields are not rendered.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: percent: (v) => `${Number(v).toFixed(1)}%`, currency: (v) => `$${Number(v).toLocaleString()}`, bps: (v) => `${v}bps`

/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Responsive grid of cards with name top-left, ring-styled colored badge (change %) top-right, and large stat + 'from {previousStat}' reference side by side below.
bestFor: Monthly fund performance cards showing current NAV with prior-month reference and a ring badge for return direction
Quarterly earnings dashboard combining current EPS with prior quarter comparison and a positive/negative badge
Asset class returns panel showing current period return alongside prior-period baseline with ring badge
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when there is no prior-period comparison — the 'from' label will be blank and the layout has wasted space; use kpi-card-02 instead.

keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of MetricItem objects. Identical data shape to kpi-card-05 but badge style differs.

/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Responsive grid of cards with name top, large stat + optional 'from {previousStat}' inline, and an arrow icon + change % + 'from previous month' footer row.
bestFor: Monthly P&L report where each metric shows current value, prior-month baseline, and a footer row explicitly stating 'from previous month'
Subscription metrics (MRR, churn, new activations) with explicit month-over-month footer context for executive reporting
Risk exposure summary with current/prior absolute values and arrow-driven directional change footer
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when the comparison period is not monthly — the footer text is hardcoded as 'from previous month' and will be misleading for weekly or quarterly comparisons.

keyOptionalProps.cols: number — default: 3 — controls responsive grid columns (1, 2, 3, 4, or 5)
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — formats the stat/value before display
keyOptionalProps.previousStat: string | number (on each MetricItem) — shown as 'from {previousStat}' inline next to the stat
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of MetricItem objects. Each object uses: `name` (string), `stat` or `value` (current primary metric), `change` (string, shown in footer next to arrow), `changeType` ('positive' | 'negative' — drives arrow direction and color), `previousStat` (string | number, optional, shown inline). The footer always reads 'from previous month'. The `href` field is not rendered.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: currency: (v) => `$${Number(v).toLocaleString()}`, percent: (v) => `${Number(v).toFixed(1)}%`

    { name: 'New Subscriptions', stat: '$3.4M', previousStat: '$2.8M', change: '21.4%', changeType: 'positive' },
  ]}
  cols={3}
/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Responsive grid of cards with a colored vertical accent bar on the left edge, metric name + change text on the same row, and large stat below.
bestFor: Asset class breakdown cards (Equities, Fixed Income, Alternatives) with per-segment color coding via left accent bar
Sector performance summary where each sector card (Technology, Healthcare, Energy) has a distinct brand color accent
Multi-strategy fund dashboard where each strategy has a unique accent color for visual differentiation
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when all metrics share the same color — the accent bar adds no value without color differentiation; use kpi-card-01 instead.

keyOptionalProps.cols: number — default: 3 — controls responsive grid columns (1, 2, 3, 4, or 5)
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — formats the stat/value before display
keyOptionalProps.color: string (on each MetricItem) — Tailwind bg class for the accent bar, e.g. 'bg-blue-500' (default: 'bg-blue-500')
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of MetricItem objects. Each object uses: `name` (string), `stat` or `value` (primary metric), `change` (string, shown right-aligned on name row), `color` (string, Tailwind bg-class for the left accent bar e.g. 'bg-emerald-500'). The `changeType` field is not used — the change text is plain gray regardless.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: percent: (v) => `${Number(v).toFixed(1)}%`, currency: (v) => `$${Number(v).toLocaleString()}`
kpi-cards.snippet:
<KpiCard09
  metrics={[
    { name: 'Equities', stat: '$1.24B', change: '+8.3%', color: 'bg-blue-500' },
    { name: 'Fixed Income', stat: '$820M', change: '+2.1%', color: 'bg-emerald-500' },
    { name: 'Alternatives', stat: '$340M', change: '-1.4%', color: 'bg-violet-500' },
  ]}
  cols={3}
/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Responsive grid of cards with metric name, large stat, and a status badge at the bottom showing within/observe/critical with the allowed range.
bestFor: Risk limit monitoring panel showing current exposure per bucket (e.g. gross leverage, sector concentration) vs allowed ranges
Regulatory compliance dashboard where each metric (LCR, NSFR, capital ratio) has a within/observe/critical traffic light
Portfolio constraint tracking (duration, beta, max position size) with instant visual status badges
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid for metrics without defined operating ranges — the badge requires a `status` and `range` field; use kpi-card-01 or kpi-card-06 instead.

keyOptionalProps.cols: number — default: 3 — controls responsive grid columns (1, 2, 3, 4, or 5)
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — formats the stat/value before display
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of ConstraintItem objects (extends MetricItem). Each object uses: `name` (string), `stat` or `value` (current metric value), `status` ('within' | 'observe' | 'critical' — drives badge color: emerald/yellow/red and icon: ✓/👁/⚠), `range` (string, the allowed range shown in badge e.g. '200-410'). The `change`, `changeType` fields are not rendered.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: percent: (v) => `${Number(v).toFixed(1)}%`, currency: (v) => `$${Number(v).toLocaleString()}`, ratio: (v) => `${Number(v).toFixed(2)}x`
kpi-cards.snippet:
<KpiCard10
  metrics={[
    { name: 'Gross Leverage', stat: '1.8x', status: 'within', range: '1.0-2.5x' },
    { name: 'Sector Concentration', stat: '38%', status: 'observe', range: '10-35%' },
    { name: 'Single Name Limit', stat: '8.2%', status: 'critical', range: '0-5%' },
  ]}
  cols={3}
/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Responsive grid of cards with metric name, large stat, and a clickable goals-achieved panel row showing a status icon, goals out of 5, and status label.
bestFor: Regional fund performance cards (Europe, Americas, Asia) showing AUM stat plus goals-achieved panel linking to regional detail
Multi-strategy portfolio overview where each strategy card shows current metric and compliance goals satisfied out of 5
Business unit KPI dashboard combining a headline metric (revenue, drawdown) with a status-gated drill-down link
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when the goals concept is irrelevant — the '3/5 goals' panel is hardcoded in label format and cannot be rephrased.

keyOptionalProps.cols: number — default: 3 — controls responsive grid columns (1, 2, 3, 4, or 5)
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — formats the stat before display
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of RegionMetadataItem objects (extends MetricItem). Each object uses: `name` (string, card label), `stat` (string, the big metric value), `goalsAchieved` (number 0-5, shown as 'N/5 goals'), `status` ('within' | 'observe' | 'critical', drives icon color in goals panel), `href` (string | undefined, wraps card in anchor tag). The `change`, `changeType` fields are not rendered.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: currency: (v) => `$${Number(v).toLocaleString()}`, percent: (v) => `${Number(v).toFixed(1)}%`
kpi-cards.snippet:
<KpiCard11
  metrics={[
    { name: 'EMEA', stat: '$42.1M', goalsAchieved: 4, status: 'within', href: '/regions/emea' },
    { name: 'Americas', stat: '$78.5M', goalsAchieved: 3, status: 'observe', href: '/regions/americas' },
    { name: 'APAC', stat: '$31.2M', goalsAchieved: 1, status: 'critical', href: '/regions/apac' },
  ]}
  cols={3}
/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Section with hardcoded 'Plan overview' header and plan-upgrade link, followed by a grid of cards each with a circular progress indicator, resource name, and 'N of M used' sub-label.
bestFor: SaaS subscription usage panel showing API calls, dashboard seats, and storage slots consumed vs plan limits
Platform tier overview for a fintech portal where workspace, report, and data connector quotas are shown as circles
Resource allocation dashboard showing fund mandate slots, analyst accounts, and model licenses consumed vs allowed
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid in any trading, investment, or financial dashboard — this card is purpose-built for SaaS subscription management (plan tiers, usage limits, upgrade CTA) and has no financial analogue. Use kpi-card-15 (progress bar) or kpi-card-10 (risk limits) for limit/quota display in financial contexts.

keyOptionalProps.cols: number — default: 3 — controls responsive grid columns (1, 2, 3, 4, or 5)
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — included in props but not used in the card's render
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of PlanMetricItem objects (extends MetricItem). Each object uses: `name` (string, resource name), `capacity` (number 0-100, percentage fill for the progress circle), `current` (number | string, how many are used), `allowed` (number | string, the plan maximum — shown as 'N of M used'). The `stat`, `value`, `change` fields are not rendered.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: capacity should be a 0-100 integer representing percentage. current/allowed are typically plain counts or storage strings.
kpi-cards.snippet:
<KpiCard12
  metrics={[
    { name: 'API Requests', capacity: 62, current: 62000, allowed: 100000 },
    { name: 'Analyst Seats', capacity: 40, current: 4, allowed: 10 },
    { name: 'Model Runs', capacity: 88, current: 440, allowed: 500 },
  ]}
  cols={3}
/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Responsive grid of cards each with a circular progress indicator + 'current / budget' label, department/category name, and an optional 'View more →' footer link.
bestFor: Departmental budget utilization showing spend vs allocation for HR, Marketing, and Operations with drill-down links
Fund expense budget tracker showing current spend vs annual budget by expense category (trading, research, admin)
Project cost monitoring where each project card shows burn rate as a progress circle with link to project detail
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when there is no budget/limit concept — the circle fill requires a 0-100 `progress` value; use kpi-card-12 for plan-style layouts or kpi-card-16 for score-style.

keyOptionalProps.cols: number — default: 3 — controls responsive grid columns (1, 2, 3, 4, or 5)
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — included in props but not used in the card render
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of BudgetMetricItem objects (extends MetricItem). Each object uses: `name` (string, appended to 'Budget' label), `progress` (number 0-100, circle fill percentage), `current` (string, current spend displayed), `budget` (string, total budget displayed — shown as 'current / budget'), `href` (string | undefined, shows 'View more →' footer link). The `stat`, `value`, `change` fields are not rendered.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: current and budget are display strings (e.g. '$250', '$1,000'). progress is a 0-100 number.
kpi-cards.snippet:
<KpiCard13
  metrics={[
    { name: 'Research', progress: 42, budget: '$500K', current: '$210K', href: '/budgets/research' },
    { name: 'Trading', progress: 71, budget: '$2M', current: '$1.42M', href: '/budgets/trading' },
    { name: 'Compliance', progress: 90, budget: '$300K', current: '$270K', href: '/budgets/compliance' },
  ]}
  cols={3}
/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Responsive grid of stock ticker cards each with company name + ticker symbol, current price (colored by direction), absolute change + percentage change, and a mini sparkline area chart.
bestFor: Equity watchlist panel for 3 stocks (e.g. AAPL, MSFT, NVDA) with sparkline price history and intraday P&L
Fund holdings KPI strip showing current NAV per share, daily change, and a 2-week price sparkline per fund
Options or futures desk summary showing underlying prices with short-term trend sparklines for quick scanning
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid for more than 4 tickers simultaneously — the shared chartData array means each card uses the same dataset; for independent per-ticker charts use kpi-card-27 instead.

keyOptionalProps.cols: number — default: 3 — controls responsive grid columns (1, 2, 3, 4, or 5)
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: metrics: Array of StockMetricItem objects. Each uses: `name` (string — must match a key in chartData), `tickerSymbol` (string, shown in parentheses), `value` (string, current price display colored by changeType), `change` (string, absolute change e.g. '+12.10'), `percentageChange` (string, percent change e.g. '+7.1%'), `changeType` ('positive' | 'negative'). chartData: shared array of objects with `date` (string) plus one numeric key per ticker name matching `item.name`. Each metric plots its own `name` key from the shared chartData array.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: value/change/percentageChange are pre-formatted display strings; no valueFormatter needed. chartData values are raw numbers.
kpi-cards.snippet:
<KpiCard14
  metrics={[
    { name: 'NVIDIA', tickerSymbol: 'NVDA', value: '$875.40', change: '+24.30', percentageChange: '+2.9%', changeType: 'positive' },
    { name: 'Apple', tickerSymbol: 'AAPL', value: '$189.20', change: '-1.80', percentageChange: '-0.9%', changeType: 'negative' },
    { name: 'Microsoft', tickerSymbol: 'MSFT', value: '$412.60', change: '+5.10', percentageChange: '+1.3%', changeType: 'positive' },
  ]}
  chartData={[
    { date: 'Dec 01', 'NVIDIA': 851, 'Apple': 191, 'Microsoft': 407 },
    { date: 'Dec 02', 'NVIDIA': 862, 'Apple': 190, 'Microsoft': 408 },
    { date: 'Dec 03', 'NVIDIA': 875, 'Apple': 189, 'Microsoft': 412 },

  cols={3}
/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Responsive grid of cards with metric name, large stat value, a horizontal progress bar, and a percentage + 'stat of limit' footnote row.
bestFor: API rate limit dashboard showing current request counts vs monthly quota with visual fill bars
Regulatory exposure limit tracking (position size %, leverage %) showing actual vs maximum allowed
Fund mandate utilization cards showing current allocation vs limit (e.g. 18% of 25% allowed in sector X)
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when there is no clear upper limit — the progress bar requires a `percentage` and `limit` value; use kpi-card-01 for simple metric display.

keyOptionalProps.cols: number — default: 3 — controls responsive grid columns (1, 2, 3, 4, or 5)
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — included in props but not used in the card render
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of QuotaMetricItem objects (extends MetricItem). Each object uses: `name` (string), `stat` (string, current value displayed as the headline), `limit` (string, maximum value shown in 'stat of limit' footnote), `percentage` (number 0-100, controls progress bar fill and percentage label). The `change`, `changeType` fields are not rendered.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: stat and limit are pre-formatted display strings (e.g. '$672', '$1,000'). percentage is a 0-100 number.
kpi-cards.snippet:
<KpiCard15
  metrics={[
    { name: 'Gross Exposure', stat: '$182M', limit: '$250M', percentage: 72.8 },
    { name: 'Net Leverage', stat: '1.4x', limit: '2.0x', percentage: 70 },
    { name: 'Single Name', stat: '3.8%', limit: '5.0%', percentage: 76 },
  ]}
  cols={3}
/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Section with header text and a color legend (0-50 red, 50-75 orange, 75-100 emerald), followed by a grid of cards each showing a score name, value out of 100, and a circular progress indicator with color matching the range.
bestFor: Model quality scorecard showing accuracy, precision, and recall as 0-100 scores with color-coded health rings
Fund manager rating cards (quantitative score, risk-adjusted return score, ESG score) with visual health indicators
Compliance health dashboard where each regulatory requirement is scored 0-100 with red/orange/green ring indicators
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid for values outside the 0-100 scale — the circle color thresholds (<50 red, 50-75 orange, ≥75 emerald) are hardcoded and will misrepresent data on different scales.

keyOptionalProps.cols: number — default: 3 — controls responsive grid columns (1, 2, 3, 4, or 5)
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — included in props but not applied in this card's render
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of ScoreMetricItem objects (extends MetricItem). Each object uses: `name` (string, metric label), `value` (number 0-100, drives both the displayed number and the circle fill color: <50 red, 50-75 orange, ≥75 emerald). The `stat`, `change`, `changeType` fields are not rendered.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: value must be a 0-100 integer. No formatter needed — the card displays 'N/100' directly.
kpi-cards.snippet:
<KpiCard16
  metrics={[
    { name: 'Model Accuracy', value: 91 },
    { name: 'Factor Stability', value: 68 },
    { name: 'Backtesting Score', value: 43 },
  ]}
  cols={3}
/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: 3-card grid with interactive hover-driven area sparklines — each card shows metric name, current value (updates on chart hover), date label, and a compact interactive area chart that updates the displayed value as the user mouses over.
bestFor: Dashboard KPI panel showing monthly users, sessions, and churn where stakeholders can hover to inspect any historical data point
Interactive performance summary where hovering the sparkline reveals exact values for any past period
Time-series metric overview (3 metrics with 12-month sparklines) where the display value follows the cursor
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when you do not have a time-series dataset to drive the sparklines — both metrics[] and chartData[] props are required.

requiredProps.metrics: SparkMetricItem[] — one entry per KPI card; each has name, chartCategory, and optional valueFormatter

keyOptionalProps.chartData: DataItem[] — defined in props but NOT actually used; the component renders its own internal dataset
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts
keyOptionalProps.indexField: string — key in chartData used as X-axis label; default: "date"

kpi-cards.dataShape: metrics: SparkMetricItem[] = [{name,chartCategory,stat?,valueFormatter?}]; chartData: {date:string,[metric:string]:number|string}[]
kpi-cards.formatterHint: No formatter needed. Internal formatters: numberFormatter for users/sessions, percentageFormatter for churn.

  ]}
/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: 3-card grid with metric name, current value + date in header row, a month-over-month percentage change badge, and an interactive area sparkline chart that updates the displayed value and badge on hover.
bestFor: Executive dashboard with hover-enabled monthly KPIs where the percentage change badge updates to reflect the hovered period vs prior month
Investor reporting panel where period-over-period change context is always visible alongside the interactive sparkline
Monthly metrics deep-dive where hovering any month shows that month's value and its exact MoM change
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when you do not have a time-series dataset to drive the sparklines — both metrics[] and chartData[] props are required.

requiredProps.metrics: SparkMetricItem[] — one entry per KPI card; each has name, chartCategory, and optional valueFormatter

kpi-cards.dataShape: metrics: SparkMetricItem[] = [{name,chartCategory,stat?,valueFormatter?}]; chartData: {date:string,[metric:string]:number|string}[]

kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when you do not have a time-series dataset to drive the sparklines — both metrics[] and chartData[] props are required.

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Responsive 3-col grid of cards where each card shows a metric name, large stat with 'bpm' unit, a 'HR zone share / BPM range' header row, and a list of activity zone rows with share percentage and range.
bestFor: Portfolio strategy zone breakdown: showing allocation share per risk zone (Defensive, Moderate, Aggressive, Speculative) with basis-point ranges
Credit rating bucket distribution where each issuer card shows average duration and bucket zone breakdown with percentage shares
Multi-metric distribution display where each metric has an average value plus sub-category zone shares and ranges
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid for metrics without a zone/bucket breakdown structure — this card requires an `activities` array with type/share/zone rows; use kpi-card-01 for simple metric display.

keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — included in props but not applied in the card render
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of HeartRateMetric objects. Each uses: `name` (string, card label), `stat` (string, headline metric displayed with 'bpm' suffix), `activities` (Array of Activity: `type` string, `share` string e.g. '25.5%', `zone` string e.g. '<126' or '126-157', `href` string). The grid is hardcoded at 3 columns responsive.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: stat is a pre-formatted display string. share is a percentage string. zone is a range string.
kpi-cards.snippet:
<KpiCard20

    },

/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: 2-col grid of cards each with metric name, large total, a CategoryBar split between two segments (cyan/violet), and a legend with count values for each segment.
bestFor: Token usage dashboard showing completion vs prompt token split per model or request type
Cost allocation card showing split between two cost centers (e.g. trading cost vs financing cost)
Binary allocation breakdown for two competing categories (long vs short exposure, buy vs sell volume)
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid for more than 2 segments — the CategoryBar colors and legend are hardcoded for exactly 2 categories ('Completion tokens' / 'Prompt tokens'); use kpi-card-25 for 3-segment breakdowns.

keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — included in props but not applied in the card render
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of CategoryItem objects. Each uses: `name` (string, card label), `total` (string, headline total displayed), `split` (Array of exactly 2 numbers summing to roughly 100 or total — used as CategoryBar values), `details` (Array of exactly 2 DetailItem: `{name: string, value: string}` — legend entries; names must be 'Completion tokens' and 'Prompt tokens' for correct color mapping). The grid is fixed at 2 columns responsive.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: total and details[].value are pre-formatted display strings. split values are raw numbers (not necessarily percentages).
kpi-cards.snippet:
<KpiCard21

        { name: 'Prompt tokens', value: '$192M' },
      ],
    },
  ]}
/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: 2-col grid of cards with metric name, large total, and a vertical stack of progress-bar breakdown rows each showing sub-category name, value, percentage, and a horizontal bar.
bestFor: Crypto portfolio breakdown cards (BTC, ETH, SOL) with holdings/staking/other sub-category bars
Asset allocation detail cards showing each asset class total with sub-allocation breakdown (core/tactical/hedge) as progress bars
Fund investor type breakdown showing total AUM per fund with retail/institutional/HNW percentage bars
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid for more than 4 sub-categories per card — the vertical stack becomes difficult to read; use a bar-chart or table instead.

keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — included in props but not applied in the card render
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of TokenMetric objects. Each uses: `name` (string, card label), `total` (string, headline total), `details` (Array of DetailItem: `{name: string, value: string, percentageValue: number 0-100}` — each item renders a progress bar row). The grid is fixed at 2 columns responsive (md breakpoint).
kpi-cards.formatterHint: total and details[].value are pre-formatted display strings. percentageValue is a 0-100 number for the progress bar fill.
kpi-cards.snippet:

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Single wide card with a total headline, subtitle, a full-width CategoryBar segment breakdown, and a 2-col sub-card grid showing each channel's share percentage and revenue with a drill-down arrow link.
bestFor: Revenue channel distribution showing total sales with Online/Retail/Partners/Wholesale segment breakdown bars and per-channel revenue
AUM source breakdown: total AUM headline with institutional/retail/HNW/family-office segment bar and individual channel cards
Fee revenue attribution showing total fees with sub-cards for management/performance/other fees and channel drill-down links
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid for more than 4-5 channels — the CategoryBar colors are mapped to the first 4 Tremor colors (blue/amber/cyan/gray) and the 2-col sub-card grid becomes crowded beyond 6 items.

keyOptionalProps.title: string — default: 'Total sales' — label above the total value
keyOptionalProps.totalSales: string — default: '$292,400' — the headline total value displayed
keyOptionalProps.subtitle: string — default: 'Sales channel distribution' — label above the CategoryBar
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — included in props but not applied
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: channels: Array of SalesChannel objects. Each uses: `channel` (string, channel name), `share` (number, percentage share used in CategoryBar values and displayed as 'X%'), `revenue` (string, pre-formatted revenue for this channel), `color` (string, Tailwind bg-class for the legend dot e.g. 'bg-blue-500'), `href` (string, link for the drill-down arrow). The shares should sum to 100.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: totalSales and channel.revenue are pre-formatted display strings. channel.share is a 0-100 number.
kpi-cards.snippet:

  ]}
/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: 3-col grid of cards each with a Remix icon in a shadowed box, metric name, large stat, colored change, and a 3-row top-breakdown list showing sub-category labels and values.
bestFor: Revenue/Expenses/Engagement breakdown cards for non-profit or investor relations dashboards showing top-3 contributing sources
Portfolio attribution summary with Revenue (top holdings), Expenses (largest cost buckets), and a style/factor metric with top-3 drivers
Fund category performance card with icon branding, headline total, and the top 3 contributing strategies with their AUM
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when the breakdown list needs more than 3 items — `top3` is an object literal whose keys become labels and only 3 key-value pairs render cleanly.

keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — included in props but not applied in the card render
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of MetricCardItem objects. Each uses: `name` (string, card label), `stat` (string, headline metric), `change` (string, delta text), `changeType` ('positive' | 'negative'), `icon` (React.ComponentType — a Remix icon component), `top3` (object: up to 3 key-value pairs where key is the sub-category label and value is number | string — numbers are formatted as '$X,XXX' unless name is 'Engagement' where '%' is appended).
kpi-cards.formatterHint: stat is a pre-formatted display string. top3 values: numbers get auto-formatted as currency or percentage depending on the card name. Provide raw numbers for top3 values.

kpi-cards.visualPattern: 3-col grid of cards each with a metric title, large value, a full-width CategoryBar with blue/gray/red colors, and a horizontal legend showing percentage + label for each of the 3 segments.
bestFor: Support ticket status cards (Current/Database/Latency) showing resolved/in-progress/escalated percentage split per metric
Operational health dashboard with resolve/editing/slow or within/neutral/breached 3-way splits per KPI
Portfolio exposure 3-way split (long/neutral/short) with percentage breakdowns per asset class or strategy
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid for 2-segment splits — use kpi-card-21 (which has purpose-built 2-segment styling); also avoid for more than 3 segments as the CategoryBar colors are hardcoded to 3 (blue/gray/red).

keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — included in props but not applied in the card render
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of MetricCard objects. The grid is 3 columns responsive.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: value is a pre-formatted display string. values[] are raw numbers (percentages summing to 100). details[].percentage is a pre-formatted string like '82%'.

kpi-cards.visualPattern: 3-col grid of cards each with a title, two labeled percentage blocks (primary + secondary with colored dots) on the left, and a large circular progress indicator on the right.
bestFor: SLA compliance cards showing within-SLA vs breached percentage with a progress circle indicating SLA attainment rate
Response time or cache performance cards with two opposing metrics (e.g. hits vs misses) and a circular health indicator
Risk limit compliance cards showing compliant vs breached percentage split with a circular dial showing net compliance
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when more than 2 labels are needed — this card is designed for exactly 2 opposing metrics (primaryLabel and secondaryLabel); use kpi-card-25 for 3-way splits.

keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — included in props but not applied in the card render
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

The grid is 3 columns responsive.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: primaryLabel.percentage and secondaryLabel.percentage are pre-formatted strings like '83.3%'. progressValue is a raw 0-100 number matching the primary percentage approximately.

kpi-cards.visualPattern: 3-col grid of cards each with a title, two labeled current values with colored dots on the left, and a responsive inline LineChart on the right showing two series over time.
bestFor: Intraday call volume vs yesterday comparison cards (current vs prior day trend lines side by side)
ETL pipeline performance cards showing processing time and data volume trends over hourly intervals
Dual-series operational KPI cards comparing two time periods or two correlated metrics (e.g. query volume today vs yesterday) with a mini line chart
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid for more than 2 series per card — each card renders exactly the labels and values in the `labels` and `values` objects; more than 2 keys will render additional label blocks without additional chart colors.

keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — included in props but not applied in the card render
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of ChartMetric objects. Each uses: `title` (string, card label), `labels` (object mapping series key to display label e.g. {Today: 'Today', Yesterday: 'Yesterday'}), `values` (object mapping series key to current value string e.g. {Today: 573, Yesterday: 451}), `data` (Array of ChartDataPoint: `{time: string, [seriesKey: string]: number}`), `categories` (string[], the series keys to plot), `colors` (string[], Tremor color names e.g. ['blue', 'gray']). The data array must contain the same series keys as categories.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: values[key] can be number or string. The LineChart uses Intl.NumberFormat internally. Chart category keys must exactly match data object keys.
kpi-cards.snippet:
<KpiCard27
  metrics={[
    {
      title: 'Trade Volume',
      labels: { Today: 'Today', Yesterday: 'Yesterday' },
      values: { Today: 4820, Yesterday: 3910 },
      data: [
        { time: '9:00', Today: 2100, Yesterday: 1800 },
        { time: '10:00', Today: 3400, Yesterday: 2600 },
        { time: '11:00', Today: 4820, Yesterday: 3910 },
      ],
      categories: ['Today', 'Yesterday'],
      colors: ['blue', 'gray'],
    },
  ]}
/>

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Two-panel layout: a 5/8-width 'Cohort Statistics' card with a 3-column grid of labeled metrics, and a 3/8-width 'Top Issues' card with a numbered ranked list of categories with percentages.
bestFor: Customer support cohort dashboard showing operational KPIs (total users, CSAT, resolution rate) alongside top issue categories by volume
Risk operations summary combining cohort-level statistics (positions, exposure, violations) with a ranked top-issues list
Fund operations dashboard showing service metrics (response time, AUM, ticket count) with a ranked breakdown of issue types
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid when you only need a single panel of metrics — the two-panel layout is rigid (5/8 stats + 3/8 issues); use kpi-card-01 or kpi-card-03 for single grids.

keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — included in props but not applied in the card render
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: metrics: a 2D array (Array of columns, each column being an Array of Metric rows). Each Metric uses: `label` (string), `value` (string, displayed as headline), `change` (string, delta text), `changeType` ('positive' | 'positive' — note: always renders in emerald). For a 3-column layout pass 3 arrays of metrics. issues: Array of IssueItem: `{category: string, totalCount: number, percentage: number}` — rendered as ranked list with percentage and count.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: metrics[n][m].value and .change are pre-formatted display strings. issues[n].totalCount is a raw number (toLocaleString applied). issues[n].percentage is a 0-100 number.
kpi-cards.snippet:
<KpiCard28
  metrics={[
    [
      { label: 'Portfolio AUM', value: '$2.41B', change: '+8.2%', changeType: 'positive' },
      { label: 'CSAT Score', value: '4.7', change: '+4%', changeType: 'positive' },

kpi-cards.visualPattern: Single 'Overview' card with a horizontal wrap of metric tiles each showing a label, a 3-bar signal-strength indicator (red/orange/emerald based on 0-1 value), a large percentage, and a fractional count.
bestFor: Sales funnel KPI overview showing lead-to-quote ratio, win probability, and project load as signal bars with percentages and fractions
Model performance overview with accuracy, recall, and precision shown as signal-strength indicators (low/medium/high)
Portfolio mandate compliance overview showing pass rates as 0-1 scores with signal bars and fraction denominators
kpi-cards.avoidWhen: Avoid for metrics without a 0-1 continuous score — the 3-bar indicator uses hardcoded thresholds (0-0.3 red, 0.3-0.7 orange, 0.7-1.0 emerald) and is meaningless for metrics on different scales.

keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number | string) => string — included in props but not applied in the card render
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

kpi-cards.dataShape: Array of Metric objects. Each uses: `label` (string, metric name), `value` (number 0-1, drives the 3-bar indicator color: <0.3 → 1 red bar, 0.3-0.7 → 2 orange bars, ≥0.7 → 3 emerald bars), `percentage` (string, displayed as the large headline e.g. '85.1%'), `fraction` (string, displayed after a dash e.g. '280/329'). All metrics render in a single horizontal wrap inside one card.
kpi-cards.formatterHint: value is a raw 0-1 decimal (e.g. 0.85 for 85%). percentage and fraction are pre-formatted display strings.
kpi-cards.snippet:
<KpiCard29
  metrics={[
    { label: 'Win Rate', value: 0.82, percentage: '82.4%', fraction: '148/180' },
    { label: 'Alpha Generation', value: 0.61, percentage: '61.3%', fraction: '49/80' },
    { label: 'Drawdown Control', value: 0.28, percentage: '28.1%', fraction: '9/32' },
  ]}
/>

bar-charts.visualPattern: Vertical grouped bar chart inside a card with a toggle switch at the bottom that switches between a single current-period series and a two-series year-over-year comparison view.
bestFor: Monthly fund NAV or AUM comparing current year vs prior year with a toggle to reveal historical context
Quarterly trading P&L current vs same-period last year with single-click comparison toggle
Monthly revenue run-rate where the analyst wants to optionally overlay the prior-year baseline bar
bar-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid when you need more than 2 comparison series visible simultaneously — the toggle supports only two category sets; use bar-chart-03 (static multi-series with inline legend) instead.

keyOptionalProps.data: BarChartDataItem[] — default: [] — array of time-series data objects
keyOptionalProps.indexField: string — default: 'date' — key used as x-axis
keyOptionalProps.defaultCategories: string[] — default: ['This Year'] — categories shown when toggle is OFF
keyOptionalProps.comparisonCategories: string[] — default: ['Last Year', 'This Year'] — categories shown when toggle is ON
keyOptionalProps.comparisonLabel: string — default: 'Show same period last year' — label for the toggle switch
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number) => string — default: defaultCurrencyFormatter
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

bar-charts.dataShape: Array of objects where each object has the `indexField` key (default 'date': string) plus numeric keys for every series name used in both `defaultCategories` and `comparisonCategories`. Example shape: { date: string, 'This Year': number, 'Last Year': number }. All objects must have the same set of keys.
bar-charts.formatterHint: currency: (v) => new Intl.NumberFormat('en-US', { style: 'currency', currency: 'USD', notation: 'compact' }).format(v), percent: (v) => `${v.toFixed(1)}%`, bps: (v) => `${v}bps`
bar-charts.snippet:
<BarChart01
  data={[

    { date: 'Jun 25', 'This Year': 121000, 'Last Year': 103500 },

  description="Current year vs prior year monthly revenue comparison"
  valueFormatter={(v) =>
    new Intl.NumberFormat('en-US', { style: 'currency', currency: 'USD', notation: 'compact' }).format(v)
  }
  comparisonLabel="Show prior year"
/>

bar-charts.visualPattern: Stacked bar chart inside a card with a top summary badge grid (color dot + series name + formatted total) above the chart and no in-chart legend.
bestFor: Multi-region AUM breakdown over 12 months with Europe/Asia/North America stacked bars and aggregate totals in badge grid
Sector allocation monthly flows (Equities/Fixed Income/Alternatives) stacked to show overall fund size with per-sector totals
Quarterly revenue contribution by product line stacked bar with pre-computed totals shown as badges above chart
bar-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid when you need a percent-composition view where bars must sum to 100% — pass type='percent', or use donut-chart-01 for proportional slices without a time axis.

keyOptionalProps.tabs: TabItem[] — summary badges: Array<{name: string, value: string, color: string}> — displayed above chart as metric pills
keyOptionalProps.data: BarChartDataItem[] — default: []
keyOptionalProps.categories: string[] — default: ['Europe','Asia','North America'] — series keys in data
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number) => string — default: defaultCurrencyFormatter
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

bar-charts.dataShape: Array of objects with `date` (string) as index and one numeric key per series matching the `categories` array (e.g. { date: 'Jan 23', Europe: 68560, Asia: 28560, 'North America': 34940 }). The `tabs` prop is a parallel summary array of { name: string (must match a categories entry), value: string (pre-formatted aggregate total), color: string (Tailwind bg class, e.g. 'bg-blue-500 dark:bg-blue-500') }.
bar-charts.formatterHint: currency: (v) => new Intl.NumberFormat('en-US', { style: 'currency', currency: 'USD', notation: 'compact' }).format(v), millions: (v) => `$${(v/1e6).toFixed(1)}M`

bar-charts.visualPattern: Grouped bar chart inside a card with an inline summary legend above the chart showing colored squares, labels, large pre-formatted values, and optional change badges.
bestFor: YoY fund P&L comparison with '$0.8M this year' vs '$0.7M last year' and '+16%' change badge visible above the grouped bars
Quarterly AUM inflows vs outflows with headline totals and net-change annotation above chart
Monthly expense actual vs budget with colored totals and variance percentage displayed in the summary above
bar-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid for more than 3 series — the horizontal summary list overflows; use bar-chart-02 (badge grid with stacking) for dense multi-series data.

keyOptionalProps.data: BarChartDataItem[] — default: []
keyOptionalProps.summary: SummaryMetric[] — default: prefilled — Array<{label: string, value: string, color: string, change?: string}> shown above chart
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number) => string — default: defaultCurrencyFormatter
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

bar-charts.dataShape: Array of objects with `date` (string) index and numeric keys matching `categories` (e.g. { date: 'Jan 25', 'This Year': 85000, 'Last Year': 72000 }). The `summary` prop is an array of { label: string, value: string (pre-formatted, e.g. '$0.8M'), color: string (Tailwind bg class), change?: string (optional badge text, e.g. '+16%') }.
bar-charts.formatterHint: currency: (v) => new Intl.NumberFormat('en-US', { style: 'currency', currency: 'USD', notation: 'compact' }).format(v), millions: (v) => `$${(v/1e6).toFixed(1)}M`

bar-charts.visualPattern: Tabbed card where each tab uses a bordered style with a large total number and colored change delta in the tab header; the active tab shows a stacked bar chart and a categorized detail list below.
bestFor: Regional payment breakdown (Europe/Asia) with per-tab Successful/Refunded/Fraudulent transaction counts and totals in tab headers
Fund trade breakdown by geography with buy/sell/cancel subcategory detail list and per-region aggregate in bordered tab headers
Multi-exchange order flow where each exchange tab shows total orders with change vs previous period and a per-order-type breakdown list
bar-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid when you have more than 3-4 tabs — the border-tab style with delta text becomes a horizontal scroll; use bar-chart-05 (pill tabs, no per-tab totals) instead.

keyOptionalProps.summary: SummaryTabItem[] — the core data: per-tab time-series, detail items, and header stats
keyOptionalProps.categories: string[] — series keys rendered in each tab's bar chart
keyOptionalProps.detailItemColorMap: Record<string, string> — maps detail item name to Tailwind bg class for color dot
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number) => string — default: defaultCurrencyFormatter
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

bar-charts.dataShape: `summary` is an array of SummaryTabItem: { name: string (tab label), total: number (large headline in tab), change: string (e.g. '+2.3%'), changeType: 'positive'|'negative', periodLabel?: string (e.g. 'vs previous period'), data: Array<{date: string, [series: string]: string|number}>, details: Array<{name: string, value: number}> }. The `data` array per tab uses the same `categories` keys. The `details` array lists subcategory totals shown below the chart.

bar-charts.visualPattern: Tabbed card with standard pill-style tabs above a stacked bar chart, and a colored-dot detail list below the chart for each tab's subcategory breakdown.
bestFor: Three-region payment volume breakdown (Europe/North America/Asia) with subcategory detail list under each tab's stacked chart
Portfolio strategy attribution showing Equity/Credit/Macro subcategory splits per strategy tab with item-level totals
Multi-exchange settlement breakdown with Settled/Failed/Pending per exchange, switching via pill tabs with detail counts below
bar-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid when you need a total count and change delta visible in the tab header itself — use bar-chart-04 (border tabs with headline totals) instead.

keyOptionalProps.summary: SummaryTabItem[] — Array<{name: string, data: BarChartDataItem[], details: Array<{name: string, value: number}>}>
keyOptionalProps.categories: string[] — series keys in each tab's data
keyOptionalProps.detailItemColorMap: Record<string, string> — maps item name to Tailwind bg class
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number) => string — default: defaultCurrencyFormatter
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

bar-charts.dataShape: `summary` is an array of SummaryTabItem (no total/change fields): { name: string (tab label), data: Array<{date: string, [series: string]: string|number}>, details: Array<{name: string, value: number}> }. Each tab's data and details arrays share the same `categories` series keys. Use `detailItemColorMap` to assign colors to detail items by name.

bar-charts.visualPattern: RadioCardGroup selector row above a single-series bar chart; selecting a radio card dynamically filters the chart to display only that card's data series.
bestFor: Regional sales selector where clicking a region card (showing its monthly total) updates the bar chart to show only that region's 24-month series
Fund share class switcher showing one share class NAV series at a time with monthly totals in radio cards
Currency-switched revenue view where each radio card represents USD/EUR/GBP and selecting one updates the bar chart accordingly
bar-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid when you need to compare two or more series side-by-side simultaneously — the radio selector forces single-series display; use bar-chart-01 (toggle) or bar-chart-03 (grouped) instead.

keyOptionalProps.tabs: TabItem[] — Array<{name: string, value: string}> — radio card items; name must match a key in data objects
keyOptionalProps.data: BarChartDataItem[] — default: []
keyOptionalProps.title: string — optional section title
keyOptionalProps.categories: string[] — default: ['Value'] — single-item array; the chart series is renamed to this label
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number) => string — default: defaultCurrencyFormatter
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

bar-charts.dataShape: `data` is an array of objects with the `indexField` key (default 'date': string) plus one numeric key per tab name (e.g. { date: 'Jan 25', Europe: 68560, Asia: 28560, 'North America': 34940 }). Each `tabs` entry has { name: string (must exactly match a data key), value: string (pre-formatted aggregate shown in radio card) }. The chart maps the selected tab's name to `categories[0]` for display.
bar-charts.formatterHint: currency: (v) => new Intl.NumberFormat('en-US', { style: 'currency', currency: 'USD', notation: 'compact' }).format(v), millions: (v) => `$${(v/1e6).toFixed(1)}M`

bar-charts.visualPattern: Animated CountUp metric headline above a click-interactive bar chart; clicking a bar animates the headline to that bar's value, clicking a legend label animates it to that category's average.
bestFor: Monthly portfolio return bar chart where clicking a specific month animates the headline to reveal that month's return vs overall average
Strategy performance bars where clicking a legend label shows the average BPS for that strategy in the animated counter
Multi-asset class volume bars for a trading desk where bar-clicking highlights a day's fill volume with animated transition
bar-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid when interaction is not important — the CountUp animation adds visual noise to static reports; use bar-chart-03 for a static grouped chart with a clean summary legend.

keyOptionalProps.data: DataPoint[] — default: [] — Array<{[indexField]: string, [category: string]: number}>

keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

The CountUp headline initially shows the grand average across all categories and all data points. bar-charts.visualPattern: Full-bleed card split into a description header section and a data section with colored-square summary totals followed by a stacked bar chart below.
bestFor: Daily API request volume vs error count over a 31-day period with total successful-requests and total-errors in the summary above the stacked bars
Fund trade execution: successful fills vs rejections per day with cumulative totals shown in summary squares above the chart
Order management system volume vs failed orders over a month with headline aggregate metrics above the colored stacked bars
bar-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid for more than 3 series — the summary list of colored squares wraps awkwardly with many items; use bar-chart-09 (tabbed multi-view with its own summaries per tab) instead.

keyOptionalProps.data: BarChartDataItem[] — default: []

bar-charts.visualPattern: Full-bleed card with a solid pill-style tab switcher top-left and a pulsing 'Updated just now' live indicator top-right; each tab shows its own summary metric list and stacked bar chart.
bestFor: API health dashboard with a 'Ratio' tab (success vs error rate) and a 'Projects' tab (breakdown by project) switchable without page reload
Fund analytics portal switching between 'Strategy Attribution' (Long/Short/Market Neutral) and 'Geography Breakdown' (Americas/EMEA/APAC) views
Trade surveillance toggling between 'Error Ratio' view and 'Source Breakdown' view with independent summaries per tab
bar-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid when each tab needs to display its own aggregate total prominently in the tab button — the solid pill tabs show only text labels; use bar-chart-04 (border tabs with headline totals) instead.

keyOptionalProps.tabs: TabConfig[] — core data; each tab has its own data, categories, colors, summary, and optional type/indexField
