bar-charts.visualPattern: Vertical stack of multiple synchronized single-series mini bar charts inside one card, each track showing its own colored headline value, metric label, and a compact bar chart (syncId='sync' for aligned hover).
bestFor: Website analytics panel: page views, unique visitors, and bounce rate as three synchronized mini bar charts stacked vertically for a 30-day period
Fund multi-metric strip: daily AUM, trade volume, and NAV as independent synchronized sparkline bars with headline values in one scrollable card
Trading system KPIs: fills per day, rejections per day, and average latency as three stacked mini bar charts with colored headline values
bar-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid when you need to compare values across the metric tracks against each other in the same chart area — the tracks are vertically stacked with independent scales; use bar-chart-03 or bar-chart-08 for a combined multi-series view.

keyOptionalProps.metrics: MetricConfig[] — core data: Array<{title: string, description: string, data: BarChartDataItem[], metric: string, color: AvailableChartColorsKeys, value: string}>
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number) => string — default: integer formatter (applied to chart tooltips)
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

bar-charts.dataShape: `metrics` is an array of MetricConfig objects, one per chart track: { title: string (label above chart), description: string, data: Array<{date: string, [metric: string]: string|number}>, metric: string (the data key to chart, must match a key in each data object), color: AvailableChartColorsKeys (controls bar and headline text color), value: string (pre-formatted headline value displayed prominently) }. Each track has its own data array and may have different lengths and value ranges.
bar-charts.snippet:
<BarChart10
  metrics={[
    {
      title: 'Daily Trades',
      description: '',
      data: [
        { date: '01', 'Daily Trades': 1240 },
        { date: '02', 'Daily Trades': 1380 },
        { date: '03', 'Daily Trades': 1195 },
      ],
      metric: 'Daily Trades',
      color: 'blue',
      value: '3,815',
    },
    {
      title: 'Rejections',
      description: '',
      data: [
        { date: '01', Rejections: 12 },
        { date: '02', Rejections: 8 },
        { date: '03', Rejections: 21 },
      ],
      metric: 'Rejections',
      color: 'red',
      value: '41',
    },
  ]}
  valueFormatter={(n) => Intl.NumberFormat('us').format(n).toString()}
/>

bar-charts.visualPattern: Single-series vertical bar chart without a card wrapper, with a labeled Y-axis (e.g. 'Competition density (%)') and amber bars showing 0-100% density values over time.
bestFor: Bidder density by month for an auction analytics platform showing competition level as a percentage over 8 months
Market concentration score (0-100%) for a sector or asset class charted monthly with a Y-axis label
Keyword auction density for a fintech analytics product tracking how competitive a market is period-by-period
bar-charts.avoidWhen: DEPRECATED — use bar-chart-12 with a single category and type="default". bar-chart-11 is a strict subset of bar-chart-12; bar-chart-11 prop `metric` maps to `categories={[metric]}` in bar-chart-12.

keyOptionalProps.data: BarChartDataItem[] — default: []
keyOptionalProps.metric: string — default: 'Density' — key in data objects to chart
keyOptionalProps.yAxisLabel: string — default: 'Competition density (%)' — label on Y-axis
keyOptionalProps.barCategoryGap: string — default: '30%' — controls spacing between bars
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number) => string — default: formats as integer percentage (e.g. 0.75 → '75%')
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

bar-charts.dataShape: Array of objects with `indexField` key (default 'date': string) and a single numeric key matching the `metric` prop (default 'Density'). Values should be 0-1 floats (e.g. 0.75 represents 75%). The default formatter renders them as whole-number percentages using: formatPercentage({ number: value, decimals: 0 }).
bar-charts.snippet:
<BarChart11
  data={[
    { date: 'Jan 25', Density: 0.72 },
    { date: 'Feb 25', Density: 0.45 },
    { date: 'Mar 25', Density: 0.88 },
    { date: 'Apr 25', Density: 0.61 },
    { date: 'May 25', Density: 0.33 },
    { date: 'Jun 25', Density: 0.79 },
  ]}
  title="Credit Market Bidder Density"
  description="Monthly competition level measured by number and size of bidders"
  metric="Density"
  yAxisLabel="Competition density (%)"
  valueFormatter={(v) => `${(v * 100).toFixed(0)}%`}
/>

bar-charts.visualPattern: Multi-series vertical bar chart without a card wrapper, supporting default grouped, stacked, or 100%-stacked percent modes, with an optional labeled Y-axis.
bestFor: ESG criteria: 'Addressed' vs 'Unrealized' as 100%-stacked percent bars showing monthly coverage rate with a Y-axis label '% of criteria addressed'
Strategy attribution monthly bars showing Long/Short/Macro absolute contribution stacked — comparing total alpha over time
Multi-asset allocation shift over quarters: Equities/Fixed Income/Alternatives grouped bars showing rebalancing trends
bar-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid for single-series density or ratio data with a percentage Y-axis — use bar-chart-11 which has dedicated amber styling and built-in percentage formatter for that pattern.

keyOptionalProps.data: BarChartDataItem[] — default: []
keyOptionalProps.categories: string[] — if omitted, auto-derived from first data object keys excluding indexField
keyOptionalProps.colors: AvailableChartColorsKeys[] — if omitted, all categories default to 'blue'
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number) => string — default: (v) => `${v}`
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

bar-charts.dataShape: Array of objects with `indexField` key (default 'date': string) and one numeric key per series. If `categories` is not supplied, all non-indexField keys are used as series in the order they appear in the first data object. For percent mode (ESG use case), values represent counts and the chart normalizes them to 100% — e.g. { date: 'Jan 24', Addressed: 8, Unrealized: 12 } stacks to show 40% / 60%.
bar-charts.snippet:
<BarChart12
  data={[
    { date: 'Jan 25', Addressed: 9, Unrealized: 11 },
    { date: 'Feb 25', Addressed: 11, Unrealized: 9 },
    { date: 'Mar 25', Addressed: 7, Unrealized: 13 },
    { date: 'Apr 25', Addressed: 13, Unrealized: 7 },
    { date: 'May 25', Addressed: 10, Unrealized: 10 },
    { date: 'Jun 25', Addressed: 14, Unrealized: 6 },
  ]}
  title="ESG Criteria Coverage"
  description="Monthly ESG criteria addressed vs unrealized in portfolio biddings"
  categories={['Addressed', 'Unrealized']}
  colors={['emerald', 'cyan']}
  type="percent"
  yAxisLabel="% of criteria addressed"
  barCategoryGap="30%"
  valueFormatter={(v) => `${v}%`}
/>

bar-lists.visualPattern: Card with a title/subtitle header row, collapsible bar list below truncated at ~260px, and a Show more / Show less toggle button over a gradient overlay at the bottom.
bestFor: Top portfolio holdings by market value (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL…) ranked from largest to smallest
Sector allocation weights where each sector name maps to a dollar AUM or percentage weight
Fixed income ladder showing bond positions ranked by face value with a formatted currency suffix
bar-lists.avoidWhen: Avoid when the list is always short (≤5 items) and will never need expansion — use bar-list-06 if you also need tab switching, or bar-list-07 if click-to-detail is needed.

keyOptionalProps.data: BarListItem[] — default: [] — array of {name, value} items rendered as proportional bars
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number) => string — default: (v) => `${v}` — formats the numeric value label on each bar
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

bar-lists.dataShape: Array of BarListItem objects, each with `name` (string, the label shown on the bar row) and `value` (number, drives both bar width proportion and the formatted label). Items are rendered in the array order; sort descending before passing to get a ranked list. No structural constraints on count, but more than ~12 items can make the collapsed view feel heavy.
bar-lists.snippet:
<BarList01
  title="Top Holdings"
  subtitle="Market Value"
  data={[
    { name: 'AAPL', value: 4820 },
    { name: 'MSFT', value: 3910 },
    { name: 'GOOGL', value: 2740 },
    { name: 'AMZN', value: 2310 },
    { name: 'NVDA', value: 1980 },
    { name: 'META', value: 1650 },
    { name: 'TSLA', value: 1420 },
    { name: 'BRK.B', value: 1100 },
  ]}
  valueFormatter={(v) => `$${(v / 1000).toFixed(1)}B`}
/>

bar-lists.visualPattern: Card showing top 5 bar rows with a gradient overlay and Show more button that opens a full-screen Dialog with a live search input and infinitely scrollable bar list.
bestFor: Large universe of securities (20+ names) where users need to search by ticker or fund name within the expanded modal
Broker or venue flow breakdown ranked by notional traded, with search to quickly locate a specific counterparty
ETF constituent holdings where the user drills into one holding by typing its ticker in the search dialog
bar-lists.avoidWhen: Avoid when the dataset is always ≤5 items and a dialog is unnecessary overhead — use bar-list-01 without the dialog complexity instead.

keyOptionalProps.data: BarListItem[] — default: [] — full list; only first 5 shown in card; all available in expanded dialog
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number) => string — default: (v) => `${v}` — formats bar value labels
keyOptionalProps.title: string — default: 'Top Pages' — card and dialog header label
keyOptionalProps.subtitle: string — default: 'Visitors' — right-side column header label
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

bar-lists.dataShape: Array of BarListItem objects, each with `name` (string, searchable label) and `value` (number). The card always shows only the first 5 items; the dialog shows all items filtered by the user's search query against `name`. Sort descending before passing for correct ranking.
bar-lists.snippet:
<BarList02
  title="Top Counterparties"
  subtitle="Notional ($M)"
  data={[
    { name: 'Goldman Sachs', value: 8420 },
    { name: 'Morgan Stanley', value: 6710 },
    { name: 'JPMorgan', value: 5980 },
    { name: 'Citadel Securities', value: 4230 },
    { name: 'Virtu Financial', value: 3810 },
    { name: 'Barclays', value: 3200 },
    { name: 'Deutsche Bank', value: 2750 },
    { name: 'UBS', value: 2100 },
  ]}
  valueFormatter={(v) => `$${(v / 1000).toFixed(1)}B`}
/>

bar-lists.visualPattern: Card with a large aggregate metric header (label above, bold value below), then a title/subtitle subheader row, top 5 proportional bar rows, and a Show more button opening a searchable Dialog.
bestFor: Fund AUM breakdown by investor type (Institutional, Retail, Pension…) with total AUM displayed as the hero metric above the list
Revenue by product line ranked by contribution, with total portfolio revenue highlighted at the top of the card
Active position count by sector with total open positions as the headline figure and per-sector bars below
bar-lists.avoidWhen: Avoid when there is no meaningful aggregate metric to display at the top — use bar-list-02 instead for a cleaner list-only layout with dialog search.

keyOptionalProps.data: BarListItem[] — default: [] — full ranked list; top 5 shown in card
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number) => string — default: (v) => `${v}` — applied to bar value labels
keyOptionalProps.title: string — default: 'Top pages' — section subheader
keyOptionalProps.subtitle: string — default: 'Visitors' — right column label
keyOptionalProps.headerValue: string — default: '113,061' — the large bold aggregate metric string displayed at top
keyOptionalProps.headerLabel: string — default: 'Website visitors' — small label above the aggregate metric
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

bar-lists.dataShape: Array of BarListItem objects, each with `name` (string) and `value` (number). Only the first 5 are shown in the card; all are searchable in the expanded Dialog. The `headerValue` is a pre-formatted string (e.g. '$2.41B') — you must compute and format it yourself before passing.
bar-lists.snippet:
<BarList03
  headerLabel="Total AUM"
  headerValue="$4.82B"
  title="Top Investors"
  subtitle="Allocation ($M)"
  data={[
    { name: 'Sovereign Wealth Fund', value: 1240 },
    { name: 'Pension Funds', value: 980 },
    { name: 'Endowments', value: 720 },
    { name: 'Family Offices', value: 610 },
    { name: 'Insurance', value: 490 },
    { name: 'Retail', value: 380 },
  ]}
  valueFormatter={(v) => `$${v}M`}
/>

bar-lists.visualPattern: Card with a title, a segmented progress bar showing two status counts with percentages (left side highlighted in blue, right in gray), a two-column ID/date list of first 5 items, and a Show more button opening a searchable Dialog.
bestFor: Trade settlement status dashboard showing settled vs pending trade IDs with timestamps, where fulfilled = settled
Order fill status across a trading session: filled (blue) to unfilled (gray) ratio with order IDs and submission times
Reconciliation queue showing matched vs unmatched entries with a vertical-bar style legend
bar-lists.avoidWhen: Avoid when no binary status split (fulfilled/open) is meaningful for the data — use bar-list-01 or bar-list-02 for plain ranked lists.

keyOptionalProps.data: ListItem[] — default: [] — each item must have the keys referenced by nameField and dateField
keyOptionalProps.progressValue: number — default: 78.2 — the filled portion (%) of the progress bar
keyOptionalProps.fulfilledCount: number — default: 1543 — integer count shown next to the fulfilled label
keyOptionalProps.fulfilledPercent: number — default: 78.2 — percentage shown in parentheses for fulfilled side
keyOptionalProps.openCount: number — default: 431 — integer count for the open/unfilled side
keyOptionalProps.openPercent: number — default: 21.8 — percentage for the open side
keyOptionalProps.statusColumnLabel: string — default: 'Open orders' — left column header in the list
keyOptionalProps.dateColumnLabel: string — default: 'Order date' — right column header in the list
keyOptionalProps.nameField: string — default: 'name' — key in ListItem used as the left column
keyOptionalProps.dateField: string — default: 'date' — key in ListItem used as the right column
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

bar-lists.dataShape: Array of generic ListItem objects (Record<string, string|number>). Each item must contain at minimum the keys specified by `nameField` (default 'name') and `dateField` (default 'date'). Typically: `{ name: 'TRD-1234', date: '2025-02-14 09:42' }`. The progress bar and counts are separate scalar props — they are not derived from the data array.
bar-lists.formatterHint: No valueFormatter prop; values in data are rendered as-is (strings). Format numeric counts via fulfilledCount/openCount props directly.
bar-lists.snippet:
<BarList04
  title="Settlement Status"
  progressValue={82.4}
  fulfilledCount={2060}
  fulfilledPercent={82.4}
  openCount={440}
  openPercent={17.6}
  fulfilledLabel="Settled"
  openLabel="Pending"
  statusColumnLabel="Trade ID"
  dateColumnLabel="Trade Date"
  nameField="name"
  dateField="date"
  data={[
    { name: 'TRD-8821', date: '27/02/2025 09:42' },
    { name: 'TRD-8819', date: '27/02/2025 10:15' },
    { name: 'TRD-8814', date: '26/02/2025 14:33' },
    { name: 'TRD-8807', date: '26/02/2025 15:50' },
    { name: 'TRD-8799', date: '25/02/2025 11:22' },
  ]}
/>

bar-lists.visualPattern: Card with a title, segmented progress bar, square-dot legend (blue/gray) with counts, a two-column ID/date list, and a Show more Dialog with search — identical data model to bar-list-04 but uses square colored dots instead of vertical bars for the legend.
bestFor: Trade execution dashboard: filled (blue dot) vs working (gray dot) orders with order IDs and submission timestamps
Corporate action processing queue: processed vs pending entitlements with event IDs and effective dates
Margin call tracking: met (blue) vs outstanding (gray) calls with account IDs and call dates
bar-lists.avoidWhen: Avoid when you need the vertical-bar legend style — use bar-list-04 instead. Both components are functionally equivalent; choose based on visual design preference.

keyOptionalProps.data: ListItem[] — default: [] — items rendered using nameField and dateField keys
keyOptionalProps.title: string — default: 'Order overview'
keyOptionalProps.progressValue: number — default: 78.2 — filled portion % of the progress bar
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

bar-lists.dataShape: Identical to bar-list-04: array of ListItem (Record<string, string|number>) with keys matching `nameField` and `dateField`. The progress bar stats (counts, percents) are separate scalar props and are not computed from the data array.
bar-lists.formatterHint: No valueFormatter; data values render as strings. Pass pre-formatted strings in the data array for display.
bar-lists.snippet:
<BarList05
  title="Order Execution Status"
  progressValue={64.8}
  fulfilledCount={1620}
  fulfilledPercent={64.8}
  openCount={880}
  openPercent={35.2}
  fulfilledLabel="Filled"
  openLabel="Working"
  statusColumnLabel="Order ID"
  dateColumnLabel="Submitted"
  nameField="name"
  dateField="date"
  data={[
    { name: 'ORD-4421', date: '27/02/2025 09:01' },
    { name: 'ORD-4418', date: '27/02/2025 09:34' },
    { name: 'ORD-4415', date: '26/02/2025 15:12' },
    { name: 'ORD-4409', date: '26/02/2025 11:45' },
    { name: 'ORD-4402', date: '25/02/2025 14:28' },
  ]}
/>

bar-lists.visualPattern: Card with a title on the left and solid-style tab switcher on the right; switching tabs swaps the entire bar list below with no pagination or dialog.
bestFor: Portfolio breakdown switchable between asset class (Equity, Fixed Income, Alternatives) and geography (US, EM, Europe) tabs
Revenue attribution switchable between sector and instrument type dimensions for the same time period
Risk factor exposure switchable between market risk and credit risk breakdowns in one compact card
bar-lists.avoidWhen: Avoid when data exceeds ~8 items per tab and users need to search — use bar-list-02 or bar-list-03 with dialog instead. Also avoid when you need more than ~4 tabs (visual overflow).

keyOptionalProps.tabs: TabData[] — default: [] — each tab has a name (tab label) and data (BarListItem[])
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number) => string — default: (v) => `${v}` — applied uniformly to all tabs
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

bar-lists.dataShape: Array of TabData objects: `{ name: string; data: BarListItem[] }`. The `name` field is both the tab button label and the Tabs value key. Each tab's `data` is an array of `{ name: string; value: number }`. All tabs share the same `valueFormatter`. The first tab is active by default.
bar-lists.snippet:
<BarList06
  title="Portfolio Exposure"
  tabs={[
    {
      name: 'Asset Class',
      data: [
        { name: 'Equity', value: 4820 },
        { name: 'Fixed Income', value: 3100 },
        { name: 'Alternatives', value: 1240 },
        { name: 'Cash', value: 640 },
      ],
    },
    {
      name: 'Geography',
      data: [
        { name: 'North America', value: 5200 },
        { name: 'Europe', value: 2310 },
        { name: 'Asia Pacific', value: 1890 },
        { name: 'Emerging Markets', value: 400 },
      ],
    },
  ]}
  valueFormatter={(v) => `$${(v / 1000).toFixed(1)}B`}
/>

bar-lists.visualPattern: Two stacked elements: a dashed-border summary metric card above showing total or selected-item value, and a Card below with a clickable bar list where clicking a row drills into that item's value in the summary card with a Clear selection button.
bestFor: Portfolio sector drill-down: total portfolio value by default, drill into any sector (e.g. Technology) to see its isolated allocation
Fund redemption tracker: total redemptions YTD, click any investor to see their individual redemption amount
Revenue by strategy: aggregate fund revenue at top, click any strategy (Long/Short, Macro, Quant) to isolate its contribution
bar-lists.avoidWhen: Avoid when click-to-select interactivity is not needed — use bar-list-01 or bar-list-06 for simpler non-interactive ranked lists.

keyOptionalProps.data: BarListItem[] — default: [] — the bar items; clicking one updates the summary card above
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number) => string — default: (v) => `${v}` — applied to both the summary card metric and bar value labels
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

bar-lists.dataShape: Array of BarListItem objects, each with `name` (string, displayed as the bar label and as the selected-item label in the summary card) and `value` (number, drives bar proportion and is displayed via valueFormatter in the summary card). When no item is selected, the summary shows the sum of all values. Clicking the same bar again deselects it.
bar-lists.snippet:
<BarList07
  title="Allocation by Strategy"
  data={[
    { name: 'Long / Short Equity', value: 4820 },
    { name: 'Global Macro', value: 3100 },
    { name: 'Quantitative', value: 2410 },
    { name: 'Event Driven', value: 1640 },
    { name: 'Fixed Income Arb', value: 980 },
  ]}
  valueFormatter={(v) => `$${(v / 1000).toFixed(1)}B`}
/>

donut-charts.visualPattern: Centered donut chart with a card wrapper, followed by a two-column header row (category / amount) and a divider list of segments showing a colored square swatch, name, formatted value, and optional share-percentage badge.
bestFor: Expense budget breakdown by category (Travel, IT, Training) showing absolute spend and percentage share of total
Fund AUM allocation by asset class (Equities, Fixed Income, Alternatives) with dollar amounts and weight percentages
Revenue concentration by client segment with each segment's contribution value and its share of total revenue
donut-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid when data needs to be sliced across multiple dimensions simultaneously — use donut-chart-03 or donut-chart-04 (which add tab switching) instead.

requiredProps.title: string
requiredProps.data: Array<{ name: string; value: number; share?: string; color?: string }>

keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number) => string — default: '$' + Intl.NumberFormat formatter — formats each item's value in the list
keyOptionalProps.categoryLabel: string — default: 'Category' — left header label above the legend list
keyOptionalProps.amountLabel: string — default: 'Amount / Share' — right header label above the legend list
keyOptionalProps.showLabel: boolean — default: true — shows center total label inside donut
keyOptionalProps.showTooltip: boolean — default: false — enables hover tooltip on donut segments
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

donut-charts.dataShape: Array of objects each with `name` (string, segment label), `value` (number, raw numeric amount driving segment size and formatted display), `share` (optional string like '32.1%' shown as a gray badge in the list row), and `color` (optional Tailwind background class string like 'bg-cyan-500 dark:bg-cyan-500' used for the legend swatch — if omitted the swatch renders gray). Items are displayed in array order. No strict limit but 5–7 items is ideal for readability.
donut-charts.snippet:
<DonutChart01
  title="Portfolio Allocation by Asset Class"
  data={[
    { name: 'Equities', value: 1240000, share: '49.6%', color: 'bg-cyan-500 dark:bg-cyan-500' },
    { name: 'Fixed Income', value: 750000, share: '30.0%', color: 'bg-blue-500 dark:bg-blue-500' },
    { name: 'Real Estate', value: 310000, share: '12.4%', color: 'bg-emerald-500 dark:bg-emerald-500' },
    { name: 'Alternatives', value: 150000, share: '6.0%', color: 'bg-violet-500 dark:bg-violet-500' },
    { name: 'Cash', value: 50000, share: '2.0%', color: 'bg-fuchsia-500 dark:bg-fuchsia-500' },
  ]}
  valueFormatter={(v) => '$' + Intl.NumberFormat('us').format(v)}
  categoryLabel="Asset Class"
  amountLabel="Value / Weight"
/>

donut-charts.visualPattern: Three nested concentric ProgressCircle rings (blue outer, green middle, amber inner) with a center numeric value, beside a static hardcoded sidebar list of three regions each with two sub-region detail rows.
bestFor: Three-tier target attainment visual: e.g. showing North America (76%), Asia (66%), Europe (57%) progress toward sales quotas simultaneously
Multi-region coverage rate comparison: displaying portfolio coverage ratios for three geographic regions as nested progress rings
Three-level risk utilization gauge: showing VaR utilization across market risk (outer), credit risk (middle), and liquidity risk (inner) limits
donut-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid when the region labels or sub-region details need to be dynamic — the sidebar data is hardcoded in the component; use donut-chart-01 with a custom layout for arbitrary segmentation instead.

keyOptionalProps.outerValue: number — default: 76.1 — percentage (0–100) for the outer blue ring
keyOptionalProps.middleValue: number — default: 66.4 — percentage (0–100) for the middle green ring
keyOptionalProps.innerValue: number — default: 56.5 — percentage (0–100) for the inner amber ring
keyOptionalProps.outerRadius: number — default: 70 — pixel radius of outer ring
keyOptionalProps.middleRadius: number — default: 60 — pixel radius of middle ring
keyOptionalProps.innerRadius: number — default: 50 — pixel radius of inner ring
keyOptionalProps.strokeWidth: number — default: 7 — ring stroke width in pixels
keyOptionalProps.displayUnit: string — default: '/10' — smaller suffix after center value
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

donut-charts.dataShape: No dynamic data array — the three sidebar region items (Europe, Asia, North America) with their sub-regions are hardcoded inside the component. Pass ring percentages via `outerValue`, `middleValue`, `innerValue` props and the center label via `displayValue`/`displayUnit`.
donut-charts.formatterHint: No valueFormatter prop — values are passed as plain numbers (0–100 for ring fill) and as strings for `displayValue`/`displayUnit`.
donut-charts.snippet:
<DonutChart02
  title="Regional Sales Attainment"
  outerValue={78.5}
  middleValue={64.2}
  innerValue={52.1}
  displayValue="6.5"
  displayUnit="/10"
/>

donut-charts.visualPattern: Card with a title and optional description, tab bar to switch between breakdown views, donut chart centered below tabs, then a two-column header row and a divider legend list with colored square swatches per tab.
bestFor: Expense breakdown switchable between category view and employee view, showing spend concentration per tab with share badges
Fund cost analysis with tabs for expense type (management fees, admin, legal) and fund class (Class A, Class B, Class C)
Revenue breakdown toggleable between client segment and product line, each tab showing amounts and percentage contribution
donut-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid when only one data dimension exists and no tab switching is needed — use donut-chart-01 for a simpler single-view donut with legend.

requiredProps.title: string
requiredProps.tabSets: Array<{ id: string; label: string; data: Array<{ name: string; value: number; share?: string; color?: string }> }>

keyOptionalProps.description: string — default: placeholder text — subtitle shown below title
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (value: number) => string — default: '$' + Intl.NumberFormat formatter — formats values in the legend
keyOptionalProps.amountLabel: string — default: 'Amount / Share' — right column header in legend
keyOptionalProps.colors: AvailableChartColorsKeys[] — default: ['cyan','blue','emerald','violet','fuchsia'] — segment colors applied across all tabs
keyOptionalProps.showLabel: boolean — default: true — shows center total inside donut
keyOptionalProps.showTooltip: boolean — default: false — enables segment hover tooltip
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

donut-charts.dataShape: Array of TabDataSet objects each with `id` (string, unique tab identifier), `label` (string, displayed on the tab trigger), and `data` (array of items). line-charts.visualPattern: Compact card with a small multi-series line chart (no y-axis, start/end x-labels only) above a colored-dot legend row list showing each series name and its aggregate value.
bestFor: Trailing 12-month revenue breakdown by channel (Organic, Sponsored, Affiliate) with period totals
Monthly NAV trend across 2-3 fund share classes with total AUM per class below the chart
Quarterly fee revenue by product line with color-coded summary rows and annual totals
line-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid when the user needs to compare exact data points or hover for detail — the chart is decorative/summary only; use line-chart-06 (tabs + full y-axis) instead.

keyOptionalProps.colors: string[] — default: ['blue','violet','fuchsia'] — Tremor color names for each series

keyOptionalProps.indexField: string — default: 'date' — key used as x-axis

keyOptionalProps.rangeTabs: RangeTab[] — optional compact range selector rendered as solid pill tabs above the chart; each tab has `name` (display label e.g. '1W') and `dataRange` (pre-sliced data array for that window); when omitted the full `data` array is rendered as-is

line-charts.dataShape: An array of objects where each object represents one time period. One key must match `indexField` (default 'date', e.g. 'Jan 23'). All remaining keys must match the strings in `categories` exactly (e.g. 'Organic', 'Sponsored', 'Affiliate'). Summary array is a separate parallel structure with `name` matching each category and `value` as a pre-computed aggregate number (e.g. annual total). Max 3 series for legibility given the compact chart height. Optionally `rangeTabs`: Array<{ name: string; dataRange: Array<Record<string, any>> }> — one entry per period pill tab; each `dataRange` must share the same key schema as the main `data` array.

Single bond or commodity price trend with a dual-column metrics panel (coupon, yield, duration, maturity)
line-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid when comparing 2+ securities — this component renders a single series only; use line-chart-07 or line-chart-08 for multi-asset comparison.

line-charts.visualPattern: Full-bleed card with headline value and change badge, a tab strip (e.g. 7d / 30d / Max) each loading a different data slice into a multi-series line chart, and a dual-column portfolio summary grid at the bottom.
bestFor: Portfolio total return vs benchmark (S&P 500) with switchable 7d/30d/max time windows and a 6-metric stats panel
Fund NAV vs market index with tab-selectable date ranges and invested/cashflow/gain summary breakdown
Active strategy P&L vs passive benchmark with user-selectable lookback and realized/unrealized/dividends metrics
line-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid when there is only one time range to display — the tabs add complexity without value; use line-chart-02 or line-chart-07 instead.

keyOptionalProps.categories: string[] — default: ['Portfolio','Market Index'] — must match keys in tab dataRange objects
keyOptionalProps.defaultTab: string — defaults to tabs[2].name — which tab is active on mount
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (num: number) => string — default: Intl locale format
keyOptionalProps.periodLabel: string — default: 'Past 24 hours' — label shown beside the change value
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

line-charts.dataShape: `data` is the full dataset. Each `TabDefinition` has `name` (tab label string) and `dataRange` (a slice of the data array for that window). Each data object has the `indexField` key plus one numeric key per `categories` entry (e.g. 'Portfolio', 'Market Index'). `summary` is an array of `ChartSummaryItem` with `name` (string), `value` (pre-formatted string), and `changeType` ('positive' | 'negative' | null — null renders neutral). Provide exactly 6 summary items for the 3+3 dual-column layout.
line-charts.snippet:
<LineChart06
  data={data}
  headline="VWAP Strategy vs S&P 500"
  headlineValue="$142,830"
  change="+$8,420 (6.3%)"
  changeType="positive"
  categories={['Portfolio', 'Market Index']}
  colors={['blue', 'cyan']}
  tabs={[
    { name: 'Last 7d', dataRange: data.slice(-7) },
    { name: 'Last 30d', dataRange: data.slice(-30) },
    { name: 'Max.', dataRange: data },
  ]}
  summary={[
    { name: 'Portfolio value', value: '$142,830', changeType: null },
    { name: 'Invested', value: '$98,200', changeType: null },
    { name: 'Cashflow', value: '$102,400', changeType: null },
    { name: 'Price gain', value: '+$44,630', changeType: 'positive' },
    { name: 'Realized', value: '+$3,120', changeType: 'positive' },
    { name: 'Dividends', value: '+$1,840', changeType: 'positive' },
  ]}
  valueFormatter={(v) => `$${v.toFixed(0)}`}
/>

line-charts.visualPattern: Single card with a 3-column chart area and a 1-column vertical sidebar listing each series name and latest value with a colored bar, plus a compare-asset button below the sidebar. Optionally renders solid pill range tabs (e.g. 1W/1M/3M/1Y) above the chart when `rangeTabs` is supplied.
bestFor: Side-by-side NAV trajectory for 3 ETF share classes with latest values in the sidebar and an add-to-compare CTA
Intraday P&L curves for 3 individual positions with current mark-to-market values in the sidebar panel
Multi-fund performance comparison (Large Cap Growth, Value, Blend) with AUM values per fund in the sidebar
line-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid when the sidebar needs sub-detail lines (address, type badge) — use line-chart-03 instead; avoid when a separate card layout for the sidebar is preferred — use line-chart-08.

keyOptionalProps.categories: string[] — default: ['ETF Shares Vital','Vitainvest Core','iShares Tech Growth'] — must match data keys
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (num: number) => string
keyOptionalProps.onCompare: () => void — callback for the compare button
keyOptionalProps.rangeTabs: RangeTab[] — optional compact period selector rendered as solid pill tabs above the chart; each entry has `name` (display label e.g. '1W') and `dataRange` (pre-sliced data array for that window); when omitted the full `data` array is rendered
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

line-charts.dataShape: Array of objects with `indexField` key (e.g. 'date': 'Aug 01') and one numeric key per `categories` entry. Series key names in data must match `categories` strings exactly. `summary` is an array of `SeriesSummaryItem` with `name` (matching category string), `value` (pre-formatted display string e.g. '$17,349.30'), and `bgColor` (Tailwind background class e.g. 'bg-blue-500 dark:bg-blue-500'). One entry per series. When `rangeTabs` is used, each `dataRange` must be a pre-sliced subset of the full data with identical keys.

line-charts.visualPattern: Two-card grid layout: a large chart card (lg:col-span-2) containing the multi-series line chart, and a separate summary card (lg:col-span-1) listing series names and values with color bars and a compare button. Optionally renders solid pill range tabs above the chart card when `rangeTabs` is supplied.
bestFor: Dashboard panel comparing 3 sector ETFs (Technology, Healthcare, Financials) with the summary card sitting visually alongside the chart
Multi-asset class performance tile (Equities, Fixed Income, Alternatives) with a separate card for latest NAVs and a compare CTA
Peer group analysis of 3 mutual funds with a standalone sidebar card showing latest NAVs
line-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid when everything should be in one unified card — use line-chart-07 instead; avoid when geographic sub-labels are needed per series — use line-chart-03.

keyOptionalProps.categories: string[] — default: ['ETF Shares Vital','Vitainvest Core','iShares Tech Growth']
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (num: number) => string
keyOptionalProps.rangeTabs: RangeTab[] — optional period pill selector above the chart card; same shape as line-chart-07 (import `RangeTab` from `line-chart-07`); when omitted renders the full `data` array
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

line-charts.dataShape: Identical to line-chart-07: array with `indexField` key and numeric keys per `categories` entry. `summary` array of `SeriesSummaryItem` with `name`, `value` (pre-formatted string), and `bgColor` (Tailwind class). Unlike line-chart-07, this component renders without a wrapping outer Card — it provides its own two-card internal grid. When `rangeTabs` is used, each `dataRange` must be a pre-sliced subset with identical keys.

line-charts.visualPattern: Full-width layout with either (a) a filterbar panel containing a DateRangePicker and compare button when no `rangeTabs` are supplied, or (b) solid pill range tabs above the chart when `rangeTabs` is provided — followed by a tall multi-series line chart.
bestFor: Interactive ETF performance explorer where analysts can zoom into custom date ranges (e.g. during a volatility spike or earnings window)
Fund NAV comparison with user-selectable date window for custom attribution period analysis
Multi-asset return series where the analyst needs to isolate specific event windows (Fed decisions, earnings, macro events)
line-charts.avoidWhen: Avoid when the date index is not ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD) and you need the DateRangePicker — date filtering will silently fail with non-ISO strings. If interactivity is not needed, pass `rangeTabs` with pre-sliced data instead (no ISO requirement); for fully static display without any range control use line-chart-07 or line-chart-06.

keyOptionalProps.categories: string[] — default: ['ETF Shares Vital','Vitainvest Core','iShares Tech Growth']
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (num: number) => string
keyOptionalProps.indexField: string — default: 'date' — MUST contain ISO strings (YYYY-MM-DD) for date filtering to work
keyOptionalProps.firstAvailableDate: Date — default: new Date(2023,7,1) — lower bound for DateRangePicker
keyOptionalProps.lastAvailableDate: Date — default: new Date(2023,8,26) — upper bound for DateRangePicker
keyOptionalProps.rangeTabs: RangeTab[] — optional period pill selector; when provided, replaces the entire DateRangePicker filterbar with compact solid tabs and each `dataRange` entry is a pre-sliced subset of the full data; when omitted the full DateRangePicker filterbar is shown and ISO date strings are required for filtering
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

line-charts.dataShape: Array of objects where the `indexField` key (default 'date') must hold ISO date strings in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format when using the DateRangePicker — string comparison is used for filtering (>= startStr, <= endStr). When using `rangeTabs` instead, the data in each `dataRange` slice does not need to be ISO format since no filtering is applied. Numeric keys match `categories` entries exactly. Set `firstAvailableDate` and `lastAvailableDate` to span the full data range so the picker defaults show all data on initial render.

  valueFormatter={(v) => `$${v.toFixed(2)}`}

status-monitoring.visualPattern: Single card with service name header + status badge top-right, checkmark icon + display name + uptime percentage row, and a responsive 90/60/30-day color-coded tracker bar with date labels.
bestFor: Market data feed uptime dashboard showing 90-day operational history for feeds like equities, FX, or options pricing
API gateway SLA monitoring card for a fintech platform displaying uptime percentage and single-service status badge
Exchange connectivity health page showing per-connection 90-day availability history for prime brokerage systems
status-monitoring.avoidWhen: Avoid when you need to display incident details or a color legend alongside the tracker — use tracker-04 or tracker-05 which include an incidents list and legend; tracker-01 is display-only.

keyOptionalProps.data: Tracker01DataItem[] — default: [] — array of daily status entries driving the tracker bar colors
keyOptionalProps.displayName: string — default: 'Service' — shown next to the checkmark icon
keyOptionalProps.uptime: string — default: '99.9%' — uptime percentage displayed on the right
keyOptionalProps.uptimeLabel: string — default: 'uptime' — label appended after uptime value (e.g. 'availability', 'SLA')
keyOptionalProps.status: string — default: 'Operational' — drives the status badge color via statusColorMap
keyOptionalProps.statusColorMap: Record<string, string> — default: {Operational: emerald, Downtime: red, Degraded: yellow, Inactive: gray, Maintenance: blue} — maps status strings to Tailwind bg-color classes
keyOptionalProps.daysAgoLabel: string — default: '90 days ago' — left label beneath the tracker
keyOptionalProps.todayLabel: string — default: 'Today' — right label beneath the tracker
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

status-monitoring.dataShape: Array of Tracker01DataItem objects where each entry represents one day: `tooltip` (string, shown on hover — typically a date string like '21 Oct, 2023'), `status` (string, must match a key in statusColorMap e.g. 'Operational' | 'Downtime' | 'Degraded' | 'Inactive' | 'Maintenance'). Provide 90 items for the full history; the component automatically slices to 60 and 30 for smaller breakpoints. Any unknown status value falls back to bg-gray-300.
status-monitoring.formatterHint: No valueFormatter. uptime is a pre-formatted string (e.g. '99.9%'). tooltip is a plain date string displayed as-is on hover.

  uptimeLabel="uptime"
  status="Operational"
  data={[

    { tooltip: '2 Dec, 2023', status: 'Operational' },
    { tooltip: '3 Dec, 2023', status: 'Downtime' },
    { tooltip: '4 Dec, 2023', status: 'Operational' },
    { tooltip: '5 Dec, 2023', status: 'Degraded' },
  ]}
/>

status-monitoring.visualPattern: Single card with service name header + status badge top-right, display name + uptime row with inline 'Show details' toggle button, collapsible incident detail panel (title + narrative text), responsive 90/60/30-day tracker bar, and a color legend row.
bestFor: Exchange connectivity status page where operations teams need to read a quick incident narrative without leaving the dashboard
Prime broker API health card where the last incident root-cause text is toggled on demand alongside the 90-day uptime bar
Fintech SaaS status page for a single service (e.g. order routing, settlement) with expandable downtime explanation for clients
status-monitoring.avoidWhen: Avoid when incident details are structured as a list of dated events with durations — use tracker-04 (plain list) or tracker-05 (accordion) instead; tracker-02's detail panel holds free-form text or ReactNode only.

keyOptionalProps.data: Tracker02DataItem[] — default: [] — daily status entries for the tracker bar
keyOptionalProps.displayName: string — default: 'service.example.com' — shown next to checkmark icon
keyOptionalProps.detailsLabel: string — default: 'Show details' — toggle button label
keyOptionalProps.detailsTitle: string — default: 'Incident details' — heading inside collapsed panel
keyOptionalProps.detailsContent: React.ReactNode — default: 'No additional details available.' — body of expanded panel (string or JSX)
keyOptionalProps.statusColorMap: Record<string, string> — maps status strings to Tailwind bg-color classes
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts
keyOptionalProps.daysAgoLabel: string — default: '90 days ago' — left label beneath the tracker bar
keyOptionalProps.todayLabel: string — default: 'Today' — right label beneath the tracker bar

status-monitoring.dataShape: Array of Tracker02DataItem: `tooltip` (string, hover date label), `status` (string matching statusColorMap key). Provide up to 90 items. detailsContent accepts a string or any React node — pass incident narrative text. legendItems is an array of {status: string, label: string} pairs rendered as colored-dot pills below the tracker.
status-monitoring.formatterHint: No valueFormatter. uptime is a pre-formatted display string. detailsContent is plain text or JSX prose.

  ]}
/>

status-monitoring.visualPattern: Single card with a header label followed by monospace tag pills (data sources/feeds), a responsive 90/60/30-day color-coded tracker bar, and a footer row showing an animated pulsing green 'Running' dot + next-run countdown + 'View more' link.
bestFor: Market data pipeline health view listing tagged feeds (equities-feed, fx-rates, options-chain) with 90-day scan history and next-run countdown
ETL job status card showing tagged data sources being ingested (e.g. Bloomberg, Refinitiv, proprietary feeds) with live running indicator
Risk model batch-job monitor showing which data dependencies are being scanned with Inactive/Degraded/Downtime history and scheduled next run
status-monitoring.avoidWhen: Avoid when you need a service-name header, status badge, or uptime percentage — tracker-03 omits those in favor of a multi-source tag layout; use tracker-01 for single-service display.

keyOptionalProps.data: Tracker03DataItem[] — default: [] — daily status entries
keyOptionalProps.headerLabel: string — default: 'Monitored sources:' — label before the tag pills
keyOptionalProps.items: TagItem[] — default: [{name:'source-1'},{name:'source-2'},{name:'source-3'}] — monospace tag pills shown as source identifiers
keyOptionalProps.nextRunLabel: string — default: 'Next run: 1 hour' — shown after the '/' separator
keyOptionalProps.viewMoreLabel: string — default: 'View more →' — footer link text
keyOptionalProps.statusColorMap: Record<string, string> — default: {Operational: emerald, Downtime: red, Degraded: amber, Inactive: gray}
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

status-monitoring.dataShape: Array of Tracker03DataItem: `tooltip` (string, hover label), `status` (string matching statusColorMap key — 'Operational' | 'Downtime' | 'Degraded' | 'Inactive'). items is an array of {name: string} objects rendered as monospace pills identifying each monitored source. Provide up to 90 data entries for full history.

status-monitoring.visualPattern: Single card with service name header + status badge, checkmark icon + service name + uptime row, responsive 90/60/30-day tracker bar, color-coded legend pills, and an always-visible incidents section with a plain list of dated rows (date + duration).

Prime broker connectivity dashboard where each API endpoint card shows uptime, history bar, and a timestamped incident audit trail always visible
Compliance-grade service health card for a trading system requiring an immutable incident list with dates and durations visible without user interaction
Collapsible incident history — set collapsibleIncidents={true} to hide the log by default
status-monitoring.avoidWhen: Avoid when incident details may grow long (>5 entries) and the card will become very tall — use tracker-05 (accordion) to keep the card compact; tracker-04's list is always rendered.

keyOptionalProps.data: Tracker04DataItem[] — default: [] — daily status entries

tables.visualPattern: Static scrollable table with configurable column headers, per-column alignment (left/right/center), and optional custom cell renderer per column enabling colored status badges or any React node in cells.
bestFor: Portfolio holdings blotter listing ticker, asset class, market value, and status (settled/pending/filled) with color-coded badges per row
Trade log displaying order ID, symbol, side, quantity, and execution status where non-sortable static display is sufficient
Risk summary table showing position name, VaR, beta exposure, and gross notional with right-aligned numeric columns
tables.avoidWhen: Avoid when users need to sort columns interactively — use table-action-01 (sortable via @tanstack/react-table) instead.

keyOptionalProps.data: T[] — default: [] — the row objects to display; T must extend Record<string, unknown>
keyOptionalProps.columns: TableColumn<T>[] — default: [] — column definitions controlling key, label, alignment, and optional render
keyOptionalProps.getRowKey: (row: T, index: number) => string — default: row index — derive a stable React key per row
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

tables.dataShape: data is an array of row objects where T extends Record<string, unknown>. columns is an array of TableColumn<T> where each entry has: `key` (keyof T & string, the data field to read), `label` (string, column header text), `align` (optional 'left' | 'right' | 'center', defaults to 'left'), and `render` (optional function (value, row) => ReactNode for custom cell content). The statusBadge helper exported from the same module accepts a string value and optional variants map to render a rounded color-coded pill — built-in variants cover: live, active, inactive, downtime, executed, filled, settled, pending, open, closed, cancelled, rejected, expired, low, medium, high.
tables.formatterHint: No valueFormatter prop — use the column render function instead: render: (v) => statusBadge(String(v)), render: (v) => `$${Number(v).toLocaleString()}`, render: (v) => `${v}%`

tables.visualPattern: Sortable table powered by @tanstack/react-table with clickable column headers that toggle ascending/descending sort indicated by animated up/down chevron icon pairs, supporting any row shape via generic TData type parameter.
bestFor: Portfolio holdings table where users need to sort positions by P&L, weight, or cost basis to identify top/bottom performers
Trade blotter with sortable columns for execution time, instrument, size, and fill price enabling ad-hoc ordering by any field
Fund expense or fee ledger where analysts sort by cost center, region, or amount to identify outliers
tables.avoidWhen: Avoid for simple static display with no user interaction needed — use table-01 instead for lighter-weight rendering without the @tanstack/react-table dependency.

keyOptionalProps.data: TData[] — default: [] — row objects; TData must extend object
keyOptionalProps.columns: ColumnDef<TData>[] — default: [] — @tanstack/react-table column definitions; set enableSorting: true per column to enable sort toggle; use meta.align ('text-left' | 'text-right') for cell alignment
keyOptionalProps.initialSortId: string — default: 'workspace' — accessorKey of the column to sort by on first render
keyOptionalProps.initialSortDesc: boolean — default: false — true for descending initial sort
keyOptionalProps.className: string — optional Tailwind classes forwarded to the outermost wrapper, enabling h-full, min-h, or custom sizing in dashboard grid contexts

tables.dataShape: data is an array of TData objects (any shape). columns uses @tanstack/react-table ColumnDef<TData>: each entry requires `header` (string, column label), `accessorKey` (string, field name on TData), `enableSorting` (boolean, whether the column header is clickable), and `meta.align` (string CSS class e.g. 'text-left' or 'text-right' applied to cells). The built-in WorkspaceData type (workspace, owner, status: 'live'|'inactive', costs, region, capacity, lastEdited) is a convenient starting shape but any object type is accepted via the TData generic.
tables.formatterHint: No valueFormatter prop — format values inside the ColumnDef cell renderer: cell: (info) => `$${Number(info.getValue()).toLocaleString()}`, or cell: (info) => statusBadge(String(info.getValue()))

spark-charts.visualPattern: Vertical list of ticker rows — each row has name, description, value, change badge, and a spark area chart on the right; no card background per row
bestFor: Portfolio holdings price tracking over a short window (days)

Dashboard sidebar showing live stock prices with % change
avoidWhen: You need detailed axis labels or tooltips — use a full LineChart instead
Data has only a single series — SparkChart04 or a KPI card is cleaner
You need aggregated totals or a portfolio header — use SparkChart02

treemaps.visualPattern: Card with optional title/description, a responsive area-proportional treemap where cell size encodes value (weight, AUM, market cap) and cell fill encodes a metric via a 5-stop diverging red→slate→emerald scale (colorMode='change') or a monochrome slate→blue scale (colorMode='value'). Hierarchy group cells render with a slate border and uppercase sector label; leaf cells show name + value/change text. Floating cursor tooltip shows name, group subtitle, labelled value row, and labelled change row with strength qualifier. Gradient legend bar rendered below the chart.
bestFor: Portfolio weight heatmap — size=weight, color=daily return; shows concentration and movers simultaneously
Equity book by sector — hierarchical structure with sector group wrappers and individual holdings inside
S&P 500 / benchmark sector map — flat structure, size=market cap, color=sector return
Screening result heatmap — color cells by factor exposure or P&L, size by AUM or shares
Fund holdings overview — colorMode='value' when change data is unavailable
treemaps.avoidWhen: Avoid for fewer than 6 nodes — cell labels become unreadable. Prefer a bar chart or donut for small counts. Also avoid when precise value comparison is needed — area is hard to compare exactly.

requiredProps.data: TreemapNode[] — each node: { name, value, change?, group?, children? }; supports flat or nested hierarchy

keyOptionalProps.colorMode: 'change' | 'value' — default 'change'. 'change' uses diverging red/emerald scale driven by the `change` field; 'value' uses monochrome slate→blue scale driven by `value`
keyOptionalProps.changeRange: number — default 4. Max absolute % change at color scale endpoints (±4%). Tighten to 2–3 for sector maps, loosen to 6–10 for individual stocks on earnings days
keyOptionalProps.height: number — default 380. Pixel height of the chart area
keyOptionalProps.valueLabel: string — default 'Value'. Label shown in the tooltip value row (e.g. 'Weight', 'Mkt Cap', 'AUM')
keyOptionalProps.changeLabel: string — default 'Change'. Label shown in the tooltip change row (e.g. 'Daily Rtn', 'YTD', 'vs Bench')
keyOptionalProps.valueFormatter: (v: number) => string — formats the value shown in cell and tooltip (e.g. v => `${v.toFixed(1)}%` or v => `$${v.toFixed(0)}B`)
keyOptionalProps.changeFormatter: (v: number) => string — default adds + prefix and 2 decimal places. Override for bps or custom units
keyOptionalProps.title: string — card heading
keyOptionalProps.description: string — subtitle shown below title (date, legend hint, etc.)
keyOptionalProps.className: string — additional CSS classes on the outer Card

treemaps.dataShape: TreemapNode = { name: string; value: number; change?: number; group?: string; children?: TreemapNode[] }. `value` drives cell area. `change` drives color in 'change' mode. `group` appears as tooltip subtitle (useful for flat data where sector is not a parent node). Hierarchy: top-level nodes with `children` become sector group wrappers; leaf nodes without children render as colored cells.
treemaps.formatterHint: valueFormatter: v => `${v.toFixed(1)}%` for portfolio weights; v => `$${v.toFixed(0)}B` for market cap. changeFormatter: default covers most cases; override to v => `${v >= 0 ? '+' : ''}${(v * 100).toFixed(0)}bps` for fixed income.
treemaps.snippet:
<Treemap01
  title="Equity Book — Position Weights & Daily P&L"
  description="Feb 27, 2026 · Size = portfolio weight · Color = daily return"
  data={equityBook}
  colorMode="change"
  changeRange={4}
  valueFormatter={v => `${v.toFixed(1)}%`}
  valueLabel="Weight"
  changeLabel="Daily Rtn"
/>

heatmaps.visualPattern: Card with optional title/description, optional period-selector button strip, N×N grid with 45°-rotated column headers and row labels, cells filled with a 5-stop diverging red→slate→blue color scale, per-cell r values, distinct diagonal cells, floating cursor tooltip on hover, and a smooth gradient legend bar
bestFor: Asset correlation matrix — show which holdings diversify each other (SPY/QQQ high, TLT/SPY negative)
Factor exposure matrix — correlation between strategy returns and risk factors
Risk decomposition — pairwise correlation of portfolio sub-strategies
Sector correlation — S&P 500 GICS sector pairwise correlations over rolling window
Multi-period comparison — 1M / 3M / 6M / 1Y matrices switchable via period selector
heatmaps.avoidWhen: Avoid for fewer than 4 assets — a simple bar or table is clearer. Avoid when values are not in [-1, 1] (use a generic table instead).

requiredProps.labels: string[] — asset/factor names, length N; drives N×N grid

keyOptionalProps.matrix: number[][] — N×N symmetric correlation matrix, values in [-1,1]; required when `periods` is not supplied
keyOptionalProps.periods: HeatmapPeriod[] — array of { label, matrix, description? }; renders a period-selector button strip when length > 1
keyOptionalProps.title: string — card heading
keyOptionalProps.description: string — subtitle / annotation shown below title
keyOptionalProps.showValues: boolean — show r value inside each cell; default true
keyOptionalProps.decimals: number — decimal places for cell values; default 2
keyOptionalProps.triangle: 'full' | 'upper' | 'lower' — render all cells, upper triangle only, or lower triangle only; default 'full'
keyOptionalProps.maskBelow: number — dim cells where |r| < threshold (e.g. 0.15 to mute low-significance pairs)
keyOptionalProps.className: string — additional CSS classes on the outer Card

heatmaps.dataShape: labels: string[]; matrix?: number[][] (N×N symmetric, diagonal=1); periods?: Array<{ label: string; matrix: number[][]; description?: string }>
heatmaps.formatterHint: Cell values formatted with toFixed(decimals). Tooltip shows strength qualifier (strong/moderate/weak positive/negative) and significance warning when maskBelow is set.
heatmaps.snippet:
<Heatmap01
  title="Cross-Asset Correlation"
  labels={["SPY", "QQQ", "TLT", "GLD", "VIX"]}
  periods={[
    { label: "1M", matrix: m1m },
    { label: "3M", matrix: m3m },
    { label: "1Y", matrix: m1y }
  ]}
  triangle="lower"
  maskBelow={0.15}
/>
